title: Robust Spicy Saudi Arabian Lamb Kebabs
description: A spicy and flavorful Saudi Arabian dish featuring lamb, stilton cheese, and wok-hei, perfect for a midnight craving. This Whole30-friendly recipe is cooked to perfection on a salt block.
instructions: Preheat the salt block to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on the preheated salt block and cook for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally.
While the lamb is cooking, heat a wok or large skillet over high heat until it reaches a smoky point, achieving wok-hei.
Once the lamb is cooked, remove it from the salt block and add it to the wok, stirring to combine with the wok-hei.
Stir in the crumbled stilton cheese and cook until melted and well combined.
Serve the lamb kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
